deliverables.title: Proposta v0.2 (PT)
deliverables.description: Latest version of the proposal — Portuguese.

updates.headline: Practice Command monthly halved (multiplier re-tune, two passes)
body: Internal pricing stack re-tuned in two passes today. Pass 1: Intelligence / Compliance / Integrations 10x → 5x each. Pass 2: Compute 5x → 2x and Messaging 3x → 2x. Total stack moves from 38x → 19x — exactly half.

Monthly Practice Command prices drop accordingly: PT €1,870 → €950/mo, DE €2,805 → €1,425/mo, CH CHF 3,740 → CHF 1,900/mo. That's a ~50% cut to monthly recurring revenue per Practice Command client vs. where we started the day.

One-time build is unchanged (PT €8,000, DE €12,000, CH CHF 16,000) — implementation effort is the same; only ongoing operational load is lighter, reflecting maturing tooling (cheaper Claude usage, pre-built MCPs, productized RGPD templates, VPS consolidation, standardized messaging infra) and a more aggressive pilot entry price.
All open Healthcare quotes (Sara) and any Healthcare prospects should re-quote off the new monthly. Canonical pricing memory and project CLAUDE.md updated to match.

updates.headline: Upload-Post added to internal costs (€15/month)
body: New cost line: Upload-Post at €15/month (multi-platform social media scheduler, supports the organic content strategy Miguel is putting together).
Monthly burn moves from €300.90 → €315.90.

updates.headline: Kanban columns now cap their height
body: Each column on the Operations board (To do / In progress / Done) is now capped at ~24rem with internal vertical scroll.
Column header shows the count and a small "· scroll" hint once a column has more than 3 cards. Keeps the dashboard footprint stable no matter how Done grows.

updates.headline: Three new tasks on the board, all due 18 May
body: Jo — configure the website chatbot for our purpose (tune the system prompt + knowledge base to land on-brand).
Jo — add all updated docs to company Google Drive and share the links so dashboard deliverables become clickable (replaces the earlier Drive task on the board).
Miguel — define the social media organic content strategy (channels, cadence, pillars, examples, KPIs).

updates.headline: Operations board (internal kanban) added
body: New section at the bottom of the dashboard: an internal To do / In progress / Done kanban.
Each card shows the task title, owner chip, one-line description, due date (turns orange when due in ≤3 days), and a linked-customer tag when applicable.
Data lives in Airtable (Tasks table). Add a row with Title, Status, Owner, Description, Due Date and the dashboard picks it up on the next snapshot.
Seeded with 8 tasks summarizing current open work.

updates.headline: Pipeline view is now compact
body: Customer cards in the pipeline shrunk to one-glance: name + vertical + market + a two-line "Next" excerpt. Full details still one click away on the customer page.
Active stage columns narrowed from 256px → 208px. Empty stage columns shrank to 112px with a discrete em-dash placeholder.
Net effect: the pipeline section is roughly half as tall.

updates.headline: Recent updates + Activity log: cap to 2, expand for history
body: Both sections now show the 2 most recent entries by default with a "View N older" toggle that reveals the rest inside a max-height scrollable container.
Keeps the page short and partners focused on what changed this week, without losing the historical log.

updates.headline: Website + dashboard aligned to House Standard v1.0
body: Applied the OBLQAI Brand Identity (House Standard v1.0) across both surfaces. The three hard locks are now codified everywhere: one accent (orange #FF8449), two type families (Inter + Georgia), no AI tropes.

Website (oblqai.com): hero rewritten with Georgia Bold display headline + italic orange accent, cream ground replaces the old cool gradient, sand-paper case-study card replaces the cyan blob. Header on cream-tinted blur. Footer is now ink-ground with the typeset OBLQ·AI wordmark.
Dashboard: footer now carries the typeset OBLQ·AI wordmark per the placement rule (header carries the mark, footer carries the typeset wordmark — never both). Added the full gray-ramp tokens.
Promoted section headlines on the website to Georgia across Services, Pricing, Calculator, Methodology, Differentiators, Contact, BlogSection, and DiscoveryCTA.

updates.headline: Dashboard branding now matches the OBLQAI logo
body: Replaced the text wordmark in the header with the actual OBLQAI logo image (black 'oblq' + orange 'Ai'). Also appears small in the footer.
Retuned the accent color from the copper editorial tone (#C27C4E) to the website orange (#FF8449) so the dashboard reads as the same brand as oblqai.com.
New favicon — black square + orange dot — to match.

updates.headline: Customer detail pages + Global docs section
body: Customer cards on the pipeline are now clickable — each opens a detail page with the customer's full notes, key fields, and a list of every deliverable we have on file (proposals, decks, demos, research briefs).
Added a Global / General docs section at the bottom of the main page for reference materials partners might want (Service Blueprint v2, pricing canon, discovery process).
Moved the Recent updates section down so the main page flow is now: KPIs → Pipeline → Costs → Updates → Activities → Global docs.
Deliverables are listed by title + filename + type for now. Actual clickable file links depend on where we host the files (see next steps with Joao).

updates.headline: Full pipeline now visible on the dashboard
body: The "Customers by stage" section now shows all 11 planned stages as columns (Lead, Qualified, Discovery, Audit, Proposal, Negotiation, Won, Onboarding, Live, Churned, Lost), not just the ones with active customers.
Empty stages render as dashed placeholders so partners can see how the pipeline is structured end-to-end.
Layout switched to a horizontal scroll so the whole flow stays in one row at any screen width.

updates.headline: Dashboard goes live; partner change log begins
body: Launched the partner operations dashboard at jccosta94.github.io/oblqai-dashboard.

Reconciled Airtable CRM + costs with GitHub ops so all three surfaces agree. Stage vocabulary now matches the ObliqAI Ops project board: Lead / Qualified / Discovery / Audit / Proposal / Negotiation / Won / Onboarding / Live / Churned / Lost.
Added OpenAI Codex to internal costs at €25/month (will scale to €100 once usage steps up). Monthly burn now €300.90.
Cleared the stale €2,499 MRR on Sergio's record — Legal Efficiency Suite pricing hasn't been finalized; old per-vertical pricing no longer applies.
Pipeline now: Sergio Proposal · Sara/Renato/Zé Maria Discovery · Miranda Lead.
Next moves: Sergio follow-up · Renato + Zé Maria initial call dates pending.

costs.notes: Joao's claude.ai Max subscription. Powers all Cowork sessions for delivery work.
Hosts OpenClaw, n8n, future Hermes. Cost re-allocates across active customer engagements in monthly review.
Gemini Advanced / AI Pro — image gen and video gen.
Business tier. hello@oblqai.com email + DKIM/SPF/MX on oblqai.com domain.
Hosting for oblqai.com on the Spark (free) tier. Tracked so the line is in place when traffic crosses paid thresholds.
Free tier — repos, Projects v2, GitHub Actions. Upgrade to Pro only if private-repo Pages is needed.
Anthropic-provided research preview — no direct cost today. May become a paid tier.
Community edition. Runtime cost is rolled into the Hostinger VPS line — recorded as €0 here to keep the inventory complete.
upload-post.com — multi-platform social media scheduler. Used for the organic content strategy Miguel is putting together.
OpenAI Codex — cloud coding agent. Current tier €25/month. Expected to scale to €100/month as usage grows; bump the Amount field when the upgrade lands.
3-year domain registration purchased 2026-05-13. Renews 2029-05-13. Amount placeholder — update with actual charge from Squarespace billing.
